title: Democrats chip away at GOP cash lead centered on Trump
description: Republicans stockpiled money from big donors in national groups heading into the fall campaign season, while individual Democrats have drawn on stronger voter enthusiasm.

title: Takeaways from the Arizona primaries: Republican 2020 election deniers advance
description: Rep. Andy Biggs, who voted to overturn the 2020 election results, won the GOP nomination for governor, while state lawmaker Alexander Kolodin, a lawyer who was penalized for his role in a lawsuit alleging election fraud, won his primary for secretary of state.

title: Mamdani says U.S. authorities should arrest Netanyahu as ‘architect of a horrific genocide’
description: The New York mayor has repeatedly denounced the Israeli leader despite criticism from other Democrats. He challenged the federal government to ensure that Netanyahu be tried for war crimes by the International Criminal Court.

title: Trump has campaigned more so far in 2026 midterms than he did at this point in 2018
description: Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.

title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
description: Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.

title: Trump-backed candidates, 2020 election deniers, win Arizona primaries
description: The primaries set the field for what are expected to be heated contests for Congress and governor in a swing state this fall.

title: Latest combat deaths show risk has shifted to troops far from Iran’s borders
description: In a war dominated by drones and long-range missiles, the front line has moved to U.S. facilities far from Iran’s borders.

title: Mamdani says New York City can't arrest Netanyahu but the federal government should
description: New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.

title: Rep. Blake Moore, R-Utah, on GOP's efforts to pass budget reconciliation bill
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Congressman Blake Moore, R-Utah, about Republican efforts to move forward with a $95 billion party-line budget reconciliation bill.

title: House votes to extend government funding, aiming to avoid an election year shutdown
description: Lawmakers are hoping to avoid a third shutdown since last September. The stopgap funding measure now heads to the Senate, where it faces an uncertain future.

title: Trump meets with Lebanese leader, pledges support for peace efforts with Israel
description: The event marked the White House’s latest attempt to broker an Israeli pullout from southern Lebanon, where Israel has been fighting Hezbollah.

title: Trump hits Canada with slew of new 50% tariffs set to take effect in 30 days
description: President Trump takes aim at Canada with his latest round of tariffs hitting a variety of goods at 50%.

title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth goes to the Hill with nearly $90B funding request
description: Defense secretary Pete Hegseth will appear on the Hill on Tuesday. He's looking for a nearly $90 billion funding boost, as the Iran war once again flares.

title: Sen. Chris Murphy on the Iran war and Pete Hegseth's upcoming Senate testimony
description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Sen. Chris Murphy, D-Conn., about the escalating war in the Middle East and the testimony of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth in front of a Senate panel on Tuesday.

title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
description: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

title: Democrat set to replace Platner in Maine led state Senate with a temper
description: Troy Jackson, the presumptive Democratic nominee to challenge Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), yelled and cursed at fellow state lawmakers, interviews reveal.

title: Some Democrats condemn Mamdani over comment on possible arrest of Netanyahu
description: Some Democrats condemned the New York City mayor after he said his staff was discussing whether to arrest the Israeli prime minister when he arrives for the U.N. General Assembly.

title: A religious sect is at the heart of an old fight getting attention in a GOP race
description: Rep. Andy Biggs, the party’s likely nominee for Arizona governor, faced criticism from fellow Republicans over his handling of a small police force more than a decade ago.
